purpose: Provides an actionable timing template for how deglobalization transmits through asset classes, calibrated across multiple historical analogues
components: Phase 1: FX adjustment (initial response, currency markets move first)
Phase 2: Goods prices and margins (tariff pass-through, margin compression)
Phase 3: Capital expenditure (reshoring investment, capex cycle)
Phase 4: Productivity (learning curve, long-run structural adjustment)
current_assessment: Calibrated across multiple historical analogues with explicit similarity scores. Provides sequencing guidance for portfolio positioning across the deglobalization timeline.
limitations: Phase boundaries are blurry and can overlap
Historical analogues may not map cleanly to current institutional structure
Phase durations vary significantly across analogues
Does not account for simultaneous shocks accelerating or disrupting sequencing
